channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Lebanon's new president strikes a nationalistic tone amid regional shifts, further weakening of Hezbollah
item.description: The Lebanese parliament finally voted on a new president on Thursday following two years of deadlock. Joseph Aoun the head of the country's armed forces won a majority of the votes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's victory could give boost to Brazil's Bolsonaro's re-election hopes amid court ban on him running
item.description: Brazil's supreme court has prevented Jair Bolsonaro from running again in the 2026 presidential election, yet the former president and his team are hoping the incoming Trump administration might help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nicolás Maduro is set to be Venezuela's president until 2031
item.description: President Nicolás Maduro is about to extend his increasingly repressive rule over Venezuela until 2031.
item.dc:creator: Regina Garcia Cano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Estonia steps up patrols in the icy Baltic Sea in a show of force after suspected cable sabotage
item.description: Estonian naval ships are taking part in stepped-up patrols in the Baltic Sea by NATO countries after undersea power and communications cables have been damaged in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Emma Burrows And Hendrik Osula, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: An albatross couple shares egg duty in this captivating low-drama reality show
item.description: A popular New Zealand bird “reality show” that each year follows a pair of northern royal albatrosses as they breed and raise a chick has drawn a global audience of devoted fans in its current 10th season.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lebanon's parliament elects army commander Joseph Aoun as president, ending a 2-year deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s parliament voted Thursday to elect army commander Joseph Aoun as head of state, filling a more than two-year-long presidential vacuum.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Earth records hottest year ever in 2024 and the jump was so big it breached a key threshold
item.description: Global temperatures in 2024 soared to yet another record level, but this time it was such a big jump that Earth temporarily passed a major symbolic climate threshold.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris, Emhoff cancel trip to Singapore, Middle East, Germany amid California fires
item.description: Vice President Harris and second gentleman Doug Emhoff announced their decision to cancel their upcoming international trips on Thursday in the wake of the Los Angeles wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Details of Venezuelan opposition leader's possible arrest remain unclear amid Maduro inauguration resistance
item.description: Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado's aides claim she was detained Thursday, despite denials by the country's socialist government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trudeau: Trump talk of annexing Canada a distraction, won't happen
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Thursday he thinks President-elect Trump’s public musings about annexing the neighbor to the north is a distraction from Trump’s threat to impose steep tariffs on Canadian imports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Musk uses X livestream to amplify German far-right leader's views ahead of an election
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Tech billionaire Elon Musk livestreamed his chat with a leader of Germany's far-right party on Thursday, using the power of his social media platform, X, to amplify the party's message ahead of an upcoming national election — and raising concerns across Europe about the world's
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than 46,000 Palestinians have been killed in the Israel-Hamas war, officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — More than 46,000 Palestinians have been killed in the Israel-Hamas war, Gaza's Health Ministry said Thursday, with no end in sight to the 15-month conflict. The ministry said a total of 46,006 Palestinians have been killed and 109,378 wounded.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scientists drill nearly 2 miles down to pull 1.2 million-year-old ice core from Antarctic
item.description: An international team of scientists announced Thursday they’ve successfully drilled one of the oldest ice cores yet, penetrating nearly 2 miles (2.8 kilometers) to Antarctic bedrock to reach ice they say is at least 1.2 million years old.
item.dc:creator: PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Outgoing ICE director: Biden should have tightened border security sooner
item.description: The outgoing head of the Immigration and Customs Enforcement Agency (ICE) said in an interview Wednesday that President Biden should have tightened border security sooner.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: NOAA says La Nina ocean cooling has finally arrived, but it's weak and may cause fewer problems
item.description: A long-awaited La Nina has finally appeared, but the periodic cooling of Pacific Ocean waters is weak and unlikely to cause as many weather problems as usual, meteorologists said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An army airstrike on a village in western Myanmar has killed at least 40 people, reports say
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — An airstrike by Myanmar’s army on a village under the control of an armed ethnic minority group killed about 40 people and injured at least 20 others, officials of the group and a local charity said Thursday. They said hundreds of houses burned in a fire triggered by the bombing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japan's Ishiba visiting Malaysia and Indonesia to strengthen defense and economic ties
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba is visiting Malaysia and Indonesia as part of his effort to further strengthen defense and economic ties with Southeast Asia as threats from China rise in the region.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: An Italian journalist is freed from detention in Iran and returns home
item.description: ROME (AP) — An Italian journalist detained in Iran for three weeks was freed Wednesday and returned home, after her fate had become intertwined with that of an Iranian engineer arrested in Italy and wanted by the United States.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Russian missile attack in southern Ukraine has killed at least 13 civilians, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A daytime Russian missile attack on the southern Ukraine city of Zaporizhzhia killed at least 13 civilians and wounded about 30 others on Wednesday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuela's ultimate political survivor Nicolás Maduro faces his toughest challenge yet
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro is facing more international rebuke than at any time in his 12 years in power. The self-declared socialist is widely believed to have lost last year's election by a landslide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK lawmakers vote against inquiry into 'rape gang scandal' as Musk keeps up pressure
item.description: British lawmakers voted against an amendment to hold a national inquiry into the U.K. grooming scandal, amid international pressure from Elon Musk and others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico offers protection to famed singer after drug cartel death threats
item.description: Mexican authorities are offering protection to famed regional Mexican singer Natanael Cano and other artists after a drug cartel publicly threatened them, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bipartisan lawmakers introduce ‘Georgian Nightmare Non-Recognition Act' in warning to Putin allied government
item.description: Senior Republican and Democratic representatives will introduce a bill on Wednesday prohibiting recognition of the Georgian Dream government amid daily protests against the new government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 9 miners are trapped in a coal mine in India's northeastern Assam state
item.description: Indian authorities have summoned the army to help rescue at least nine workers who are trapped inside a flooded coal mine in the country's northeastern Assam state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tibet earthquake kills at least 126 people and leaves many trapped
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A strong earthquake shook a high-altitude region of western China and areas of Nepal on Tuesday, damaging hundreds of houses, littering streets with rubble and killing at least 126 people in Tibet. Many others were trapped as dozens of aftershocks shook the remote region.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Musk says UK's Farage should not be Reform party leader
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said that British politician Nigel Farage should not be the Reform U.K. party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: China fires shot across Trump's bow with defense sanctions
item.description: It could be a sign of a brewing tit-for-tat as the two superpowers jockey for economic and military leverage.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: At least 30 people killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza
item.description: At least 30 people, including children, were killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes overnight and into Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, NewsNation
